english: mťa-γčód-pa (mtha' gcod pa) final or definitive sentence or judgment Sch.; dei mťa γčod-pai pýir (de'i mtha' gcod pa'i phyir) in order to settle it definitely, viz. by counter-proof, Gram.; yáṅ-dag-mťa (yang dag mtha') the true end, i.e. objective truth Was. (297); the rest, remainder, re-dógs - kyi mťa spaṅ (re dogs kyi mtha' spang) having given up also the last remnant of fear and hope Glr., cf. mťá - dag (mtha' dag); mťá - ru (mtha' ru), mťar (mthar) 1. towards the end, towards the boundary or the neighbouring country; at the end etc.; mťar ťug - pa (mthar thug pa) to reach, to attain to the end, frq.; tsei mťar ťug-pai graṅs (tshe'i mthar thug pa'i grangs) the number of those that reach the (natural) end of life Dzl.; mťar - ťug - pa - méd - pa (mthar thug pa med pa) inexhaustible Dzl.; mťár-pýin-pa (mthar phyin pa) (rarely mťar - °kyil-ba (mthar 'khyil ba)) id.; also absolutely as sbst. mťár - pýin - pa (mthar phyin pa) a perfect, a holy person, a saint; mťár - ton - pa (mthar ton pa) id. (?) Mil.; mťár-byed-pa (mthar byed pa) to give a work its finish C., (Sch.: 'to destroy, demolish'?) 2. adv. lastly, finally, in conclusion Dzl., Thgy.; perh. also to the very last, wholly, altogether. 3. postp. with genit. after, behind, rgyal-rábs sum-brgyái mťar (rgyal rabs sum brgya'i mthar) after 300 royal generations Glr.; sá-mťar (sa mthar) šiṅ (shing), šiṅ (shing) is to be written after a final s, Gram. — 2. aim, purpose Cs. — 3. system, opinion Tar. 107, 4 Schf., perh. for grub-mťa (grub mtha'). Comp. and deriv. mťá-klas-pa (mtha' klas pa) Cs. = mťá-med-pa (mtha' med pa), yet v. mťas (mthas). — mťa-skór (mtha' skor) circumference, perimeter, v. dpag - tsád (dpag tshad). — mťa-°kób (mtha' 'khob) v. kób ('khob). — mťa-grú (mtha' gru) Glr. 42? — mťa-rgyás (mtha' rgyas) very wide Schr. — mťa-lčags (mtha' lcags) frame, of a mirror etc. Schr. — mťa-čag (mtha' chag) Med.? — mťa-rtén (mtha' rten) final consonant Gram.; mťa-rten-med-pa (mtha' rten med pa) ending with a vowel Gram. — mťa-ťig (mtha' thig) boundary line Sch. — mťa-ťog-ťág (mtha' thog thag) unceasing (?) Sch. — mťá-dag (mtha' dag) several, sundry; all, frq.; maṅ - tsig mťá - dag (mang tshig mtha' dag) the plural sign mťa-dag (mtha' dag) Gram. — mťa-draṅs (mtha' drangs) Gram.? — mťá ma (mtha' ma) the end, grál-gyi mťá-mai bú-mo (gral gyi mtha' ma'i bu mo) the girl at the end of the row (opp. to the middle or the other end, not necessarily to the beginning, like mǰug-ma (mjug ma)); border, hem, seam, of dresses Dzl.; deṅ mťoṅ-ba mťa-ma (deng mthong ba mtha' ma) to-day we see (him) for the last time Glr. (ťá-ma (tha ma) would be more correct, like Dzl. 292, 16). — mťa - mal - pa (mtha' mal pa) sometimes for ťa - mal - pa (tha mal pa). — mťá - mi (mtha' mi) borderer; neighbouring people. — mťá - med-pa (mtha' med pa), mťá - yas - pa (mtha' yas pa) infinite, endless. — mťa-γséb (mtha' gseb) Wdṅ.?
the bringing to an end, carrying through, persistence, perseverance Mil.
by turns, successively, Dzl.; by degrees, gradually.
Zam. = पर्यन्त, border, limit? cf. mťa (mtha') compounds.

acc. to Cs.: 'indigo', and 'mťiṅ-šiṅ (mthing shing) indigo-plant'; acc. to a Lama from Lhasa however: 1. mountain-blue (which is found, together with malachite, in the hills near Lhasa). — 2. from the resemblance: indigo-colour (whereas indigo as a substance is rams (rams)), and esp. a light sky-blue, azure; cf. mťon-mťiṅ (mthon mthing).
Lex. a certain bird; Sch.: a sort of wild duck; acc. to Pth. a smaller bird.

1. bottom, of a vessel, of the sea; floor, of a room Glr.; foundation, of a house. — 2. the lower side of a thing; inner or lower part of a thing, lág-mťil (lag mthil) (resp. pýág-mťil (phyag mthil)) the palm of the hand; lag-mťil-na (lag mthil na) in the closed hand; lag-mťil gaṅ (lag mthil gang) a closed handful; rkaṅ-mťil (rkang mthil) (resp. žabs-mťil (zhabs mthil)) the sole of the foot; lham-mťil (lham mthil) the sole of a shoe; mťil ˙bži (mthil bzhi) the palms of the hands, and the soles of the feet. — 3. the background, the far end, of a cave, a tunnel etc. — 4. C.: the centre, the principal or chief part, of a town; the principal place, chief city, capital, of a country.

1. power, force, strength, of the body, of the mind, of Buddha, of a prayer, of witchcraft etc.; ability, power or authority to do a thing; mťu daṅ ldán-pa (mthu dang ldan pa) strong, powerful, efficacious, able etc., mťu-méd (mthu med) powerless, feeble, unable; "mi za ťu mé"* (mi za thu me) C. I must eat it; bsgrub-mi-nus-mťu-méd-la soṅ (bsgrub mi nus mthu med la song) we must be able to fulfil it Mil.; mťu- (mthu-)
